Pretty much every PR I review:

0) review <URL> [codex does it's thing and finds issues]
1) is the issue clear? [if not, trash PR]
2) is this the best possible fix? [95% of the time no]
3) continue discussion, consider tradeoffs, usually rewrite PR

Following the success of the EurIPS and NeurIPS-Mexico City pilots in 2025, we are thrilled to announce two official NeurIPS 2026 satellite events for this year!

These will be held in Paris, France and Atlanta, USA, respectively, running alongside the main venue in Sydney,

The Datasets & Benchmarks track is now "Evaluation and Datasets", with an expanded scope for NeurIPS 2026!

The NeurIPS 2026 Call for Papers is now live: https://t.co/E3aLYaBNZa 

Abstracts are due May 4, 2026 (AOE), with full papers due May 6, 2026 AOE.

Teleop is so 2025. Ever since we unveiled EgoScale and the dexterity scaling law, it's been clear to us and the ecosystem that behavior cloning directly from humans is the way to break the curse of teleop. 2026 is all about scaling robot learning without robots.

A conversation I've had several times lately:
Them: "LLMs are so good for learning whatever you want — there's never been a better time to learn!"
Me: "When was the last time you learned a new domain with LLMs?"
Them: "Oh, there's no point. LLMs do a better job than me already"
